reachability_artifacts: built sworn binary interrupted during a fixture child command, with PID/process-group evidence, cancelled outcome, supervisor ownership release, and non-zero exit
same fixture release restarted through sworn run --parallel, with dispatch history proving no completed role was repeated

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; process-group mechanics and recovery checkpoints require implementer confirmation before design review.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can trust a recoverable autonomous release loop, receive durable attention pages, and monitor or safely control it from a mobile web board.

positive_scenarios: An interrupted child is gone before ownership is released.
Restart continues from the next legal role.
negative_scenarios: A pre-cancelled parallel run cannot reduce an empty result set to PASS.
A completed implement or verify role is not duplicated after restart.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Bounded cancellation and evidence-based recovery turn autonomous runs from disposable processes into controllable operations.

design_decisions.choice: Use one signal-derived process context and context-bound subprocess groups.
options: root cancellation with bounded group termination
design_decisions.rationale: Cancellation must reach every Sworn-owned descendant before the loop relinquishes ownership.

design_decisions.choice: Recover by reconciling persisted evidence before redispatch.
options: reconcile durable status, Git, and events
design_decisions.rationale: Only repository-visible evidence survives process death and can prevent duplicate work.
